commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Cubits, alluding to Judea, which was twice as long as it was broad. (Menochius) Many explain this vision and that of the woman, (ver. 7) of the Jews, (Calmet) after St. (Haydock) But is seems rather to denote the Chaldeans, whose sentence had been long pronounced, and who were punished by the Persians, and by the Greeks, as by two women. If we understand the Jews, their iniquity was chastised by the Assyrians and Chaldeans.
